reviews.review_title: Good  game, but wouldn’t play again…
reviews.review_description: So this game is kind of one of those games that you finish and never really wanna get back into. It’s like you play at once and kinda get bored of it. It’s kind of like a timeline. It never goes back so I do recommend attempting to finish this game under two hours so you can refund it. I wasn’t able to, but it is still a decent game. ￼

reviews.review_title: Nice story but holy badly done metaphors
reviews.review_description:
So I played it after first game from exploration and puzzles they're much more bigger  and quite the upgrade from the first game 

Visually I enjoyed it and the music is very nice the voice acting very nicely done and the story for the most part is heartbreaking tears in the end one 

But I would say the one that decided how to do the metaphors really needs to learn how to do it more gently because I already understood what's the hints of the story from the start but I went anyway and play it until the end 

I do recommend this game for the gameplay and the story in the music in the voice acting because overall it's very nicely done so if you can get it for cheap then yeah I recommend it
But for the length and some badly done sections and the story that you can see miles away

I will give it 3 out of 5 stars

A Fisherman's Tale 2 is a bigger game than the first one, using a totally different play mechanic. It's clear the designers set out to tell a big story about family, and they really went for it. Parts of the game are highly psychedelic with a sad but hopeful core. Basically this is a very French game with a whimsical sense of humor and a philosophical examination of family. Honestly I would have connected with the game more if the mom was different, but you can't have everything.

reviews.review_title: Don’t need the First to play the Sequel!
reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun! The game mechanics are so creative and well put-together. The hand shooting feature reminds me of the grab-pack from Poppy Playtime. The scenery is beautiful, and the puzzles are difficult, but not too hard. I’ve never played the first game, and I still understood the story. The ending got me really emotional. This is definitely a great deal for its price! I highly recommend if you enjoy puzzle games.

The story telling is entertaining, and some of the puzzles are great.  However, the hand movement mechanics can get unpredictable and EXTREMELY frustrating especially underwater in Chapter 3 (for example when trying to open a simple latch in the sunken ship the hand would float in random directions).  In summary, the game overall with the creative story telling kept me playing but the sometimes horrible hand control tracking brought the overall rating down to a mediocre rating.

The puzzle mechanics are excellent but I was captivated by the story. You can tell the story meant a lot to the developers. Great game. As others have said, you probably won’t replay this once finished but like a good book, the story stays with you. Would love more.

reviews.review_title: A great follow-up to a great game
reviews.review_description:
Thank you innerspace for always moving forward, I finished this a couple of days ago and I loved every minute of it, I highly recommend it. 

Next up, for me, maskmaker!

ps. I'm so excited for Spymaster

Saving of progress is only at predefined points. There are several times I tried to pause/quit and save my progress but the game wouldn't let me. It's frustrating enough that I don't think I'm ever going to play this again and have to redo my progress to catch up where I was.
This Is an amazing addition to the previous game and i would suggest this game and the previous one. It is a good puzzle game with a decent story that was actually cute.
Cried at the ending

I just finished A Fisherman’s Tale 2. It’s a lovely story-driven puzzle game built around perspective, spatial logic, and some delightfully strange mechanics, without combat or gore.

If, like me, you enjoyed the original A Fisherman’s Tale, this sequel will feel familiar but expanded. It isn’t quite as mind-bending as the first game, but it introduces new ways of thinking that still require you to slow down, observe carefully, and sometimes rethink what “forward” or “upside down” really means.

Gameplay centers on environmental puzzles and physical interaction rather than reflexes. One of the more surprising elements is the use of your own body parts as tool—throwing your hands, removing your head to see from a different angle, or working in reversed orientation. It sounds odd (and feels odd at first), but it becomes intuitive once you adjust, and it’s used cleverly rather than as a gimmick.

I played seated and found the game very manageable that way. There’s movement and reaching involved, but nothing frantic or physically aggressive. That said, I did get stuck a few times, not because the puzzles were unfair, but because I missed something I should have noticed. In each case, I was on the right track and just needed to slow down and look again.

This isn’t a game built around constant action or instant clarity; it expects you to think sideways, sometimes literally.

Overall, A Fisherman’s Tale 2 is imaginative, gentle, and satisfying. It respects the player’s intelligence and rewards patience and curiosity. I’m very glad I played it, and I’ll be watching closely for the next installment.
Such a fun play! Great story, hope there is a third!

Any hints in how to use the hands underwater? I'm trying to open a hatch. Hands move left-right-forward-backwards when I control them but the up-down seems to be random. I keep loosing control and the hands dissappear somewhere in the ship and I have to start over. "This is going to be a real treasure hunt, Nina, if we just get in!". We'll I'm trying but can't get the hands to move where I want them to!

It took me a bit of time to get used to the controls- throwing my head and hands off of my body etc- but once I got the hang of those I had a great time. The story is touching. I enjoyed the bright and colorful art style. The puzzles were challenging and never felt repetitive.  Great sequel to the other fisherman’s tale game!
the best game i've ever played it was so emotional and just a beautiful game and it's probably the best game you will ever play
This makes me remember the stories my dad used to tell me before he died from cancer when i was 7 and yet i still hear him telling those stories

reviews.review_title: Control system kills the fun for me
reviews.review_description: Part 1 was an absolute masterpiece and easily a 5 star game. The storytelling of part 2 is also great. But the mechanics how to control your hands are killing all the fun for me. Even knowing what to do it took me 15-30 minutes to finish some rather simple (or meant to be simple) tasks. I was fighting myself through to chapter 3, but now i give up and uninstall the game. It‘s too annoying to always be stuck with those crazy controls. Im sure there are others who can handle it better than me. But for me it has been a waste of time and money.

Such a lovely game with wonderful storytelling. It delivers a truly immersive experience and offers something refreshingly unique for players who enjoy narrative-driven games. The controls feel a bit unusual at first, but once you get used to them, they work well and add to the overall experience.
This is really nice. Just started but already love it.

LOVE LOVE LOVE LOVE THIS GAME. Both of them but #2 was just perfect. Longer and more complex and more emotional. I felt so invested in the story and so immersed in it. Had some tougher challenges that I had to put it down for and go back but it just made me more satisfied when I got through. Thanks Devs. Hoping for a 3rd!

My partner and I love a puzzle game, and a Fisherman's Tale was one of our favourite ever. The puzzles were ingenious, thoughtful and - once you'd worked them out - easy to implement. And, of course, the game was beautiful and enchanting.

This second game is also as beautiful and enchanting, and the story is engaging and moving too. But then, the 'hands'...

What starts out as a lovely idea gets more obtuse along the way. Judging by other reviews, not everyone has this problem, but for us the mechanics gradually impact the play until it becomes, literally for us, unplayable.

We spent nearly an hour, in the 'Hull' in Chapter 3, trying to get the hand onto the lock on the hatch. Try as we might, we couldn't manoeuvre the hand across the ceiling to do so - and it contuinually got stuck in the back corner, unable to move, which meant we had to start the whole task again - all the while being intermittently told by the 'hint' what we should be looking for, when we'd already found it!

As I said, we love a good puzzle, and one of the joys of gaming is trying various possible solutions, or maybe just sitting baffled, until the answer is reached. One of the very-much-lesser joys is when designers introduce a mechanic that is clunky and awkward to use, and leave the player to deal with the frustration; in the first place, it cuts the immersion, and in the worst case - ours - you may decide to give the game up, surmising that hours of your one life on earth shouldn't be taken up by dealing with difficult/faulty design choices.

So - given that there is no 'skip section' or work-around option - that's what we've done, which is a great shame. As I said, a beautiful game, spoiled somewhat...

developer_response.body:
Sorry for the delay and thanks for taking the time to give us this earnest feedback! We really appreciate the effort for playing the sequel and we're sorry to hear you had to stop because you were stuck in a specific spot after trying a few times.

The controls in the game are challenging, that's part of the concept of this sequel, but while we tried our best to soften the edges and make the gameplay accessible, we know some parts can still be frustrating.

Theoretically the difficulty level is gradual, so if you've been able to get this far into the game and play with the hand previously in the underwater level, you 'should' be able to pass the 'hull' puzzle without too much hurdle.

I encourage you to check other players on youtube to see how they handle this, then be very methodical and keep calm while doing this part to avoid false maneuvers.
We'd love to help and give you other hints so you can move forward in your adventure, so don't hesitate to reach us over email or on our discord.

reviews.review_description:
Now, stop me if you have heard this one before…what a game.
Amazing environments. Gameplay is very clever but the story oooh the story pulls you in and connects you to the fisherman’s family beginning to end. Excellent writing and allegories to real life make this continuation a worthy one! I’ll be happy to pay full price for chapter 3 if it’s this good😊

Truly love this game so far, the first one was great-this one is even better! After a long day I  feel I'm being swept away by the magic of VR to another world and enjoying the just right puzzles and immersive storyline. I will mention that I've paid a lot more for a lot less also! Highly recommend this as both a puzzle game and a simple adventure!

I just finished A fisherman‘s tale 2 last night. Possibly one of the most beautiful games I’ve ever played. 

If you like the first game, you really should play this. It’s a much bigger world, and much bigger adventure, with a much longer play time. It took me about 5 hours.

Real, Baron Von Munchausen vibes. You play as a fisherman on a crazy adventure, but you cut between him and his daughter who is packing up her father’s life who has passed away. It got a little hard to play at one point… very emotional.

You can tell whoever made this game made it with love.
The game is okay but not wow, the first part was a lot better. The hands is used in all the game makes it boring.

I remember playing Part 1 and loving it. For some reason, Part 2 never came across my feed, and I just stumbled across it yesterday. I beat it by this morning. This is exactly what VR should be used for IMO. Great Storytelling (literally), and puts you in a fantastical place you wouldn't be able to go to without a headset. My only wish is that it was longer, not because it wasn't long enough or because I felt it was shorter than what I paid for (quite the opposite of that), but because it's like putting down a great book.. Awesome job Devs. Please keep this story alive and get us that Part 3! 6/5
never have I played a video game that was so good that I cried

I loved the first game so I just had to get the sequel. I played sitting down in a very small room on a recliner and had absolutely no issues except occasionally knocking over my drink when I wasn't remembering where I placed it down. The new mechanics in this game added a very clever layer of difficulty over the first game but once you're through the "tutorial" section, it got very exciting to see what was going to happen next. I cannot recommend this game or it's first entry enough and if they make a third entry in this series I will be going all in on it as they are not only fantastic stories but the gameplay mechanics are some of the best that VR has to offer. It's games like this that don't make me feel left behind when I'm still playing on Quest 2 but if a future entry in this series is exclusive to a newer Quest headset, that would actually be a compelling reason for me to run out and buy one. Please give these games a try and I promise you will not be disappointed
This is the 2nd time i cried from a game i still am as i am writing this ( first was before your eyes) this game is great get it

This game takes you into a little adventure. Unlike many other VR games, you really feel like you are playing an extensive game that also looks beautiful. Full of puzzles and wonderfull experiences. As far as I'm concerned, I recommend this game if you like puzzle games such as Red Matter.

Absolutely loved it! Had a great time. Longer than the first, thankfully 🙌🏼 recommend playing the first one first, more of an intro to the series, to fully appreciate the story. Hopeful for a third installment 🙏🏼

…a Fisherman’s Tale 2 is brilliant. It builds on the first game’s solid base with plenty of ingenuity. The story’s sharp, fun, and delivered in truly creative ways. Puzzles hit that sweet spot: challenging  enough to make you think but not so hard you’re stuck and kills the flow the story. Every chapter brings fresh surreal gameplay. The music’s also delightful and really ties in the mood. I’m in Miami close to the water so leaving the window open to get a bit of ocean aroma in the air made it even better.  Huge congrats to the devs for pulling off an experience unlike any other in vr (except for part 1). So if you liked the first or love a story-rich adventure game, you need to check this out.

I enjoyed the 1st game so much, I bought the 2nd 1. I'm sad I completed it lol.
Only thing that would've been better is more frequent saving feature.

I loved Fisherman’s Tale 1, so I was excited to play the sequel. It’s so hard to control body parts that I keep giving up in frustration. I have a small space and sometimes have tracking issues, but this is the only game that appears to be unplayable. I struggled to make it about a tenth into the game. When I went back, all progress was gone. I’m also annoyed that each time I go back to try again, the only option it gives me is to start completely over.
The game was a good experience, but sometimes you don't no were to Go, what to do... The First part was better

reviews.review_title: Decent but not as good as the original
reviews.review_description: A Fisherman's Tale was one of the first VR games I played some years ago and I loved both the art style and the puzzle mechanics. I knew this sequel had taken a different approach to the gameplay but wanted to give it a try. Initially I enjoyed the game and found it to be fun despite the somewhat frustrating mechanic of controlling detached hands. However, I really didn't enjoy the final chapter and found it quite annoying. It's still a nice story and decent game but it simply isn't as good as the original.
The first game was a refreshing change from what I was used to. This game is just broken. Flat out infuriating. How about a hint or two if there's only one thing in the smoke scene that will work.

developer_response.body:
Hello,
Thanks for the feedback but the issue you're mentioning is not very clear to us. It would be great to understand why you feel the game is broken!
If you needed hints, they can turned on in the menu of the game, but If there's another issue, don't hesitate to write us a note at support@innerspacevr.com with more details so we can help troubleshoot and improve your experience!

reviews.review_title: It was a fun game, wish I could forget.
reviews.review_description: I love this game, it felt like a fever dream for the first couple chapters but the 4&5 one it felt like I was high on that good shi. I wish someone would make something so I can forget the games content.

Im very easily made sick by VR so keep in mind that i am unusually sensitive. I went in blind, and i couldn’t even finish the tutorial because I got dizzy and nauseated immediately. The controls involve a lot of moving around and repositioning. The first game was still enough to give me a mild headache but vr does that to me regardless. This game? I genuinely feel as though im going to be sick. The game it self seems very good as far as i made it. The voice acting is amazing, the story is engaging from the get go, i just cant with the playstyle.

developer_response.body: The gameplay can be challenging from a comfort point of view so we understand your feedback and we're sorry to hear you it felt too much for you to appreciate the experience. That said, we'd like to remind that there are many comfort settings in the game and if you crank them up, it 'should' be comfortable enough, even with sensitive users, like some of us in the team are :)

reviews.review_description:
I love the game but I am where his head is in the cage. I got the body on and the claw snaps the rope BUT how do I get the head on?? I look at YouTube walkthroughs and the head pops on and the game continues on. I try everything to get the head on. So this must be a glitch. I cannot continue. Help!

***The developer sent me instructions how to continue the games and all is good now. This was “player error” not a glitch in the game. Great game, highly recommend it!!

developer_response.body:
Hello there, thanks for trying our game! Our guess is that you may have forgotten the input to 'reelback' the head. You have to push both Y and B buttons simultaneously on your controllers to do so. We chose this combination to avoid misuse in other parts of the game. Hope that helps get you back in the adventure!
(and If the problem persists don't hesitate to reach us at support@innerspacevr.com)

reviews.review_description: Wonderful, funny, unforgetable. Thank you ☺️

There are some great ideas and concepts, but I found the controls frustrating, especially controlling the hands, swimming and that spinning room?! WTF?!
I reluctantly completed the game hoping it would get better, but I just found it tedious in the end.
I love the first game, but I can't recommend this one.

I really wanted to love this game, I had it saved to my wish list as soon as it was announced. The idea is great, the story is great but the mechanics of the fisherman being outside his body and trying to maneuver the body and his hands was just FRUSTRATING and not intuitive at all. Pressing left on the joystick would not always get a left turn result, same with right, back and forward and a lot of the game play was just moving the joystick every which way and hoping it went the direction I needed. (What am I missing here?) I found each level had a level of annoyance where I would play for an hour and get mad and give up when I couldn't get his hands or body to go the direction I wanted, I noticed this from the beginning of the game, but the water chapter (number 3) completely did me in. I think I spent probably half an hour trying to get the fish body to swim in the direction I needed him to go (often times watching him just spin and run into walls) and I suddenly realized that this is supposed to be fun. Challenging - yes. But also fun. And I can't actually remember ever having fun with this one. I don't often give up on games but this will be one that never gets finished.

The developers really hit it out of the park with this sequel to a great VR experience, A Fisherman’s Tale. Another FT was a fantastic experience. The puzzles were good, some quite challenging, and the way you need to use and interchange hands, legs, head, etc.  was an exercise in attempted coordination! I spread AFT out so I could continue to enjoy it for as long as possible and after a while I will play it again. I only look forward to another sequel at some point, maybe Nina’s Tale?

reviews.review_title: Beautiful and Fun and 25🏧 in bio
reviews.review_description:
Cute and fantastic Experience, not too hard and Great for beginning with vr
Also surprise in bio

Beautiful/touching narrative with beautiful graphics, great puzzles and gameplay!

Worth full price!

Or get it cheaper (the comma is a dot, caps matter, and remove spaces):

b i t , l y / A F T F r i e n d

What an amazing title! Played the first one, “A fisherman’s tale”, it was original, concept and all but didn’t wow me, a little repetitive gameplay. 
Came to the second and… oh my! What a fantastic experience. The story, the puzzles, the gameplay, originality, voice acting, the scenarios. I teared up at the end, not gonna lie. 
Incredible, challenging. 
10/10
A lot of fun. Nice to look at.

This is the absolute best puzzle game I've ever played.and one of my favorite parts is the cartoonist art style that it uses throughout the plot.its no doubting the best puzzle game in the market.and one I love is that it's not really that complicated when it comes to the puzzles.now that are some quite challenging parts but if you get stuck you can enable hints and it is such a help! I hope this review made you have some thoughts on buying this game.10/10

Another Fosherman s tales …..the more the merrier 
The first one was also amazing ( the Mechanics in this one however is different…owning both is awesome …this game is soooo good ( don’t listen to the critics ) this is incredibly fun , the music is incredible … WOW

reviews.review_title: Very well done puzzle game with heart
reviews.review_description: Very well done puzzle game with likeable character, fun and not overly difficult puzzles, and heartwarming story.
I love this game!  I loved the original but I think I like the sequel better.  More controls, more ways to utilize the characters, fun puzzles.  Please make another!
Beautiful story ￼￼ amazing levels and one of the best game on quest

reviews.review_title: One of the Best Things VR Has to Offer
reviews.review_description: Another Fisherman's Tale has a fantastic and far reaching story with a lot of depth. The application of VR to move the story along is brilliant - you get involved and are quite active but you're never kicked out of the story.  The visuals are gorgeous and thrilling and the designers create pathways to interact with it that seem natural and purposeful. No shooting or blood splatter for a change. Both A Fisherman's Tale and Another Fisherman's Tale are well worth your time and money. Can't wait for more from this team.

What can I say that hasn't already been said about this game? A genuine masterpiece of storytelling, filled to the brim with vibrant visuals, engaging characters and an unsurpassed charm, that is so endearing, I want to forget it all and experience it all over again for the first time. Made me smile, made me cry... 10 out of 10 must play game!
The amazing story of the old Sailor. Imaginative, clever, funny. Money well spent on a game like this.
I loved the first Fisherman’s Tale, so I couldn’t wait to get this sequel and it didn’t disappoint. Great story, graphics, voiceover and challenging puzzles. I had a blast! Well done 😀
Love this sequel. Only issue I had was at the part where you help out Captain Kenred's crew. It's a bit glitchy in one place where you throw Bob's head.  But, still fun!

developer_response.body:
Hello Honor Ella,

Thank you for your review. We are very interested by your feedback and we are curious to know what exactly you call "glitchy". If you want to share screens or feedbacks with us directly, you can join our discord: https://discord.gg/UqY5tqtqTJ

reviews.review_description: Few times are the sequels that much better. Highly recommend as a really enjoyable puzzle game. Can’t wait for the next one!

Great addition to the fisherman’s tale series. A natural progression from the first game with expanded gameplay. I found myself tearing up at the conclusion of the game story telling is 10 out of 10 with this one. only negative is puzzles felt slightly simple at times.
